w20: Compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and 16 GB RAM for efficient single-threaded application performance and general-purpose cloud workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 x86_64 architecture.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a compute-optimized server with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It utilizes the AMD EPYC 7B13 x86_64 architecture for dedicated, reliable performance in cloud environments.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 architecture. It provides 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, alongside 16 GB of RAM. The configuration includes 64 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache per core, with support for AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets. No GPU or local storage is bundled with this instance. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 25 percent, while multi-core and memory-intensive tasks show average to weak results compared to broader infrastructure standards. Memory bandwidth for large blocks averages 51.54 GB/sec for reads and 38.79 GB/sec for writes. Given the performance profile, this server is appropriate for single-threaded compute tasks, lightweight web serving, and general-purpose workloads that do not require high-concurrency multi-core processing or massive local storage capacity.
